economic_notes: First Nation operates wind energy (Bow Lake Wind Farm 60MW, Goulais Wind Farm 25MW), solar projects (Zaagate Giizis Energy Inc.), industrial park (Batchewana Band Industries Limited), and economic development through Mukwaa 2 Inc. COVID-19 impacts noted. Post year-end, a wind turbine was severely damaged causing partial shutdown of Bow Lake Wind Facility for approximately 4 months.
notes: Fiscal year 2021 (ended March 31, 2021), audited May 11, 2022 — late filing. Large increase in deferred revenue ($13.57M vs $1.65M prior year) primarily due to ISC funding deferred ($9.6M), which significantly affected recognized revenue vs confirmations ($21.99M confirmed vs $13.75M recognized). Revenue dropped sharply from 2020 ($49.8M) largely due to prior year special Nodin Kitagan LP distributions ($25.5M special + regular). Consolidation includes Batchewana First Nation Housing Authority Inc., Mukwaa Inc. (bare trustee), and 2419656 Ontario Inc. (bare trustee). Five GBEs on modified equity basis: Batchewana Band Industries Ltd., Mukwaa 2 Inc., 6597982 Manitoba Ltd., 2425771 Ontario Inc., and Zaagate Giizis Energy Inc. Term demand loans ($6.63M) reclassified separately from long-term debt. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC $13,748,532 + Province of Ontario $2,592,421 + Government of Canada $2,577,094 + North Shore Tribal Council $1,332,046 (treated as government transfer) + Government of Canada Trust Funds $18,494 + Ontario First Nations LP $463,896 approximation excluded — using ISC + Province + GoC + Trust Funds = $18,936,541; NSTC included = $20,268,587; used ISC+ON+GoC+NSTC+Trust = 19,268,587 — reported as sum of clearly identified government sources.
